note: Rotation (026) + prefix truncation (014). A prefix of the ROTATED vector is a random projection (each output dim mixes all D inputs) rather than dropping raw dims, so truncation loses far less recall. Gain is LARGER under truncation (+4.6pts at 512/C=1000) than at full bits (+0.27pts), directly fixing 014's limitation that Cohere isn't Matryoshka-trained. QPS unchanged (rotation free). New: 768-bit rot2 C=1000 = 0.983 @ 923 QPS (vs 014's 768 needing C=2000 for ~0.987 @ ~517).
